text.title: Rust project directory layout and module responsibilities
text.summary: Standard directory layout for Axum/Actix HTTP services: routes/, handlers/, services/, models/, db/, middleware/, with AppState as the dependency carrier.

rule.statement: Organize Rust HTTP services under src/ with: config.rs, error.rs, and domain-split directories: routes/, handlers/, services/, models/, db/, middleware/. Keep main.rs under ~80 lines (config load, state build, router compose, serve).
rule.rationale: A flat src/ with all code in main.rs hits the 500-line wall quickly and causes circular mod.rs imports. The split enforces a one-way dependency: routes use handlers; handlers use services; services use db; models are shared.

example.code: project/ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Cargo.toml в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Cargo.lock # committed for binaries в”њв”Ђв”Ђ src/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ main.rs # в‰¤80 lines: config, state, router, serve в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ lib.rs # library root (re-exports for integration tests) в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config.rs # Config struct, from_env() в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ error.rs # AppError enum + IntoResponse в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ routes/ # Router composition per domain в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ users.rs в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ handlers/ # Handler functions в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ users.rs в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ services/ # Business logic в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ users.rs в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ models/ # Domain structs (no DB or HTTP specifics) в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ user.rs в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ db/ # sqlx queries behind a Database struct в”‚ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ users.rs в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ middleware/ в”‚ в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mod.rs в”‚ в””в”Ђв”Ђ auth.rs в”њв”Ђв”Ђ migrations/ в””в”Ђв”Ђ tests/ # integration tests (use lib.rs exports)

rule.statement: AppState must derive Clone. Wrap all non-trivially-Clone resources (DB pool, HTTP client, Config) in Arc. Never put &str, non-Send types, or borrowed data in AppState.
rule.rationale: Axum clones AppState per request. Arc wrapping keeps the clone O(1) (atomic increment). Non-Clone or non-Send types cause compile errors that agents diagnose poorly.
